I'm auditing a spec (fix_enhancement.md) against the repo at C:\repos\oracle. The spec claims certain things were DELIBERATELY NOT done (deferred). I need to confirm they are genuinely absent (not silently half-implemented), AND check a couple things that SHOULD exist.

For each, report NOT PRESENT (confirming deferral) / PARTIALLY PRESENT / PRESENT, with file:line evidence.

DEFERRED ITEM 1 — workflow-map artifact: The spec proposes a first-class `source_workflow_maps` table/artifact (nodes_json, edges_json, paths_json, etc.). Confirm whether ANY such table/schema/worker exists. Search packages/db (schema.ts, migrations) and apps/workers for "workflow_map", "workflowMap", "source_workflow_maps". Should be ABSENT.

DEFERRED ITEM 2 — Bug G sequencing: coverage audit should run AFTER macro extraction, but spec says it still races (both fired concurrently fire-and-forget from source-outline.ts). Check apps/workers/src/trigger/source-outline.ts around where it triggers macro-relationship-extraction and source-coverage-audit. Are they still dispatched concurrently (both .trigger fire-and-forget), or is coverage now sequenced after macro? Report the actual dispatch code with line numbers.

DEFERRED ITEM 3 — Bug D gating: `packages/oracle-engines/src/macro/lifecycle.ts` — does statusForGeneratedMacroRelationship still return 'blocked_pending_support' unless ALL support claims are approved? Confirm it's unchanged (still gates the holistic layer).

DEFERRED ITEM 4 — before/after reorder: In apps/workers/src/trigger/document-ingestion.ts, confirm that processDocument still runs broad atomic extraction BEFORE triggering source-outline (i.e., the "before" macro read is still not happening; outline still runs after extraction). Report the ordering with line numbers.

ALSO CHECK these support files the spec references exist and match:
- AGENT_ERROR_LOG.md at repo root — does it exist, and does it contain ERR-001 and ERR-002?
- HANDOFF.md — does it reference AGENT_ERROR_LOG.md?

Report precisely with file paths and line numbers. Be concise.

93	const CHUNK_SIZE = 4000;
94	// Extraction-window budget: how much chunk text a single extraction call sees.
95	// Meaning lives in CONTEXT that spans chunk boundaries — a multi-paragraph SOP
96	// rule, a debated decision, and (worst case) a diagram whose meaning is entirely
97	// in cross-node arrows. Narrow windows extracted independently fragment that
98	// context, so we keep windows wide. Quote validation is unaffected —
99	// pickEvidenceChunk maps each verbatim quote back to its covering chunk.
100	const MAX_DOCUMENT_TEXT_CHARS = 24_000;
101	// Image transcriptions (especially diagrams/flowcharts) are one connected graph;
102	// give them an even wider window so the whole flow is reasoned over in one call.
103	const MAX_IMAGE_TEXT_CHARS = 32_000;
104	// Vision (Pass 1) sampling. A *thinking* VL model (e.g. qwen3-vl-*-thinking)
105	// spends a reasoning trace BEFORE the transcription text, so the output cap must
106	// be high or it truncates mid-thought (a dense diagram transcription alone is
107	// ~3-4k tokens). Temperature is non-zero on purpose: Qwen recommends AGAINST
108	// greedy decoding for thinking models (temp 0 → repetition loops); 0.6 is their
109	// guidance. Format consistency is enforced by the prompt, not by temperature.
110	const VISION_MAX_OUTPUT_TOKENS = 32_000;
111	const VISION_TEMPERATURE = 0.6;
112	// Extraction output budget. A dense window (a diagram with many handoffs) can
113	// produce dozens of claims; without a generous cap the JSON truncates mid-array,
114	// parses as a raw string, and fails the schema ("expected object").
115	const EXTRACTION_MAX_OUTPUT_TOKENS = 32_000;
116	const VERTEX_FILE_CACHE_MIN_BYTES = 10 * 1024 * 1024;

128	function buildOracleClient(): OracleAIClient {
129	  return new OracleAIClient({
130	    adapters: buildStandardAdapters(),
131	  });
132	}

134	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
135	// Text helpers
136	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
137	
138	interface TextChunk {
139	  text: string;
140	  /** Inclusive start offset into the source rawText. */
141	  start: number;
142	  /** Exclusive end offset into the source rawText. */
143	  end: number;
144	}
145	
146	type InsertedDocumentChunk = TextChunk & { id: string };
147	
148	/**
149	 * Offsets where a new chunk is ALLOWED to start, in increasing order (always
150	 * includes 0). Boundaries are semantic seams: the start of the line after a
151	 * blank-line gap, and the start of any heading line (`### …`, `#### …`, or a
152	 * numbered section like `2. ### …`). Splitting on these keeps a paragraph, a
153	 * numbered process section, or a swimlane block intact instead of severing it.
154	 */
155	function computeStructuralBoundaries(text: string): number[] {
156	  const set = new Set<number>([0]);
157	  // Alt 1: a blank-line gap → boundary is the start of the following line.
158	  // Alt 2: a newline immediately before a heading line → boundary at the heading.
159	  const re = /\n[ \t]*\n|\n(?=[ \t]*(?:\d+\.\s+)?#{1,6}\s)/g;
160	  let m: RegExpExecArray | null;
161	  while ((m = re.exec(text))) {
162	    set.add(m.index + m[0].length);
163	  }
164	  return [...set].sort((a, b) => a - b);
165	}
166	
167	/**
168	 * Structure-aware chunker. Packs text up to `chunkSize`, but cuts only at
169	 * semantic boundaries (headings / paragraph breaks; then line breaks; only a
170	 * single over-long line forces a hard char cut). Chunks stay contiguous and
171	 * non-overlapping with exact offsets into the source text, so the persisted
172	 * `document_chunks` and `pickEvidenceChunk`'s offset math keep working. This
173	 * replaces the byte-slice fallback that could sever a flowchart arrow line or a
174	 * sentence mid-word.
175	 */
176	function chunkTextStructured(text: string, chunkSize = CHUNK_SIZE): TextChunk[] {
177	  if (text.length <= chunkSize) return [{ text, start: 0, end: text.length }];
178	  const boundaries = computeStructuralBoundaries(text);
179	  const chunks: TextChunk[] = [];
180	  let cursor = 0;
181	  while (cursor < text.length) {
182	    const hardEnd = Math.min(cursor + chunkSize, text.length);
183	    if (hardEnd >= text.length) {
184	      chunks.push({ text: text.slice(cursor, text.length), start: cursor, end: text.length });
185	      break;
186	    }
187	    // Latest structural boundary that fits in (cursor, hardEnd].
188	    let cut = -1;
189	    for (const b of boundaries) {
190	      if (b > cursor && b <= hardEnd) cut = b;
191	      else if (b > hardEnd) break;
192	    }
193	    if (cut === -1) {
194	      // No structural seam fits — fall back to the latest line break, then a
195	      // hard char cut for a single line longer than chunkSize.
196	      const nl = text.slice(cursor, hardEnd).lastIndexOf('\n');
197	      cut = nl > 0 ? cursor + nl + 1 : hardEnd;
198	    }
199	    chunks.push({ text: text.slice(cursor, cut), start: cursor, end: cut });
200	    cursor = cut;
201	  }
202	  return chunks;
203	}
204	
205	function formatDocumentChunksForExtraction(chunks: Array<{ id: string; text: string }>): string {
206	  const parts: string[] = [];
207	  for (const chunk of chunks) {
208	    const header = `DOCUMENT CHUNK\nDocument Chunk ID: ${chunk.id}\nContent:\n`;
209	    const footer = '\n---\n';
210	    parts.push(`${header}${chunk.text}${footer}`);
211	  }
212	  return parts.join('\n');
213	}
214	
215	function buildDocumentChunkWindows<TChunk extends { id: string; text: string }>(
216	  chunks: TChunk[],
217	  maxChars = MAX_DOCUMENT_TEXT_CHARS,
218	): Array<{ chunks: TChunk[]; content: string }> {
219	  const windows: Array<{ chunks: TChunk[]; content: string }> = [];
220	  let current: TChunk[] = [];
221	
222	  for (const chunk of chunks) {
223	    const next = [...current, chunk];
224	    const nextContent = formatDocumentChunksForExtraction(next);
225	    if (current.length > 0 && nextContent.length > maxChars) {
226	      windows.push({ chunks: current, content: formatDocumentChunksForExtraction(current) });
227	      current = [chunk];
228	    } else {
229	      current = next;
230	    }
231	  }
232	
233	  if (current.length > 0) {
234	    windows.push({ chunks: current, content: formatDocumentChunksForExtraction(current) });
235	  }
236	
237	  return windows;
238	}

249	async function extractTextFromXlsx(buffer: Buffer): Promise<string> {
250	  const XLSX = await import('xlsx');
251	  const workbook = XLSX.read(buffer, { type: 'buffer' });
252	  const lines: string[] = [];
253	  for (const sheetName of workbook.SheetNames) {
254	    lines.push(`[Sheet: ${sheetName}]`);
255	    const sheet = workbook.Sheets[sheetName];
256	    if (sheet) {
257	      const csv = XLSX.utils.sheet_to_csv(sheet);
258	      lines.push(csv);
259	    }
260	  }
261	  return lines.join('\n');
262	}

264	async function extractTextFromDocx(buffer: Buffer): Promise<string> {
265	  // mammoth is CommonJS; normalize the namespace whether it comes through as a
266	  // default export or a flat module object.
267	  const mod = (await import('mammoth')) as unknown as {
268	    extractRawText?: (opts: { buffer: Buffer }) => Promise<{ value: string }>;
269	    default?: { extractRawText: (opts: { buffer: Buffer }) => Promise<{ value: string }> };
270	  };
271	  const extractRawText = mod.extractRawText ?? mod.default?.extractRawText;
272	  if (!extractRawText) throw new Error('mammoth.extractRawText unavailable');
273	  const result = await extractRawText({ buffer });
274	  return result.value;
275	}

277	const DOCX_MIME = 'application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document';
278	
279	const IMAGE_TRANSCRIPTION_SYSTEM = `You are a meticulous visual analyst for an operations knowledge base. You will be shown ONE image an employee uploaded. Produce a faithful, self-contained TEXT rendering of everything the image conveys. Downstream extraction reads ONLY your text — it cannot see the image — and it quotes your text verbatim, so it must be able to reconstruct both the content and the structure from your output alone.
280	
281	1. TRANSCRIBE ALL TEXT VERBATIM
282	Reproduce every piece of visible text exactly as written — labels, headings, every table cell, captions, callouts, stamps, axis labels, legends, handwriting. Never paraphrase, summarize, or normalize wording.
283	
284	2. RENDER STRUCTURE AS A TEXT TOPOLOGY (not free-form prose)
285	When the image contains a diagram, flowchart, org chart, decision tree, map, or schematic, encode its topology so relationships are unambiguous:
286	- Nodes:  [Shape/Color: "verbatim label"]   e.g.  [Blue Diamond: "Credit Check"]
287	- Edges:  CRITICAL — write EACH connection as ONE self-contained line that contains the FULL source node, the arrow (with its label/color/style if any), AND the full target node, e.g.
288	          [Blue Diamond: "Credit Check"] --(Red Arrow: "If Score < 600")--> [Gray Box: "Trigger Manual Deny Email"]
289	  NEVER split a connection across multiple lines, and NEVER put a node on one line and its outgoing arrow/target on the next line. A connection that spans two lines is wrong.
290	- If one node has several outgoing connections, write ONE complete edge line per connection and REPEAT the full source node on each line.
291	- Group swimlanes / columns / sections under headers, e.g.  ### Swimlane: Finance Department
292	Keep the verbatim label text INSIDE the node brackets so it stays exactly quotable. Downstream extraction quotes a WHOLE edge line verbatim to record a handoff, so a complete single-line edge is what lets a relationship become a validated claim — a split edge cannot be quoted and is lost.
293	
294	3. TABLES
295	Render as text preserving rows and columns — one row per line, columns separated consistently, including the header row.
296	
297	4. READING ORDER & GROUPING
298	Present content in natural reading order. Use headers to mark spatial groups, sequence, or hierarchy when they carry meaning.
299	
300	5. OPERATIONAL CONTENT
301	Ensure concrete rules, steps, routing, ownership, dates, quantities, statuses, conditions, and exceptions are explicit.
302	
303	Rules:
304	- Do NOT invent anything that is not visible. If something is unreadable or ambiguous, write [unreadable] or note the ambiguity — never guess.
305	- Output plain text only. You MAY use the simple structural markers above (### headers, the [Node] --(edge)--> [Node] notation, table rows). Do NOT use code fences, and do NOT add commentary or preamble such as "Here is".`;
306	
307	/**
308	 * Build the vision-pass user content with a PROVIDER-NEUTRAL inline image part:
309	 * `{ type:'image', mimeType, data }`. Each adapter translates this to its own
310	 * native shape AT DISPATCH (OpenAI/Qwen/DeepSeek → `image_url`, Anthropic →
311	 * base64 source block, Gemini/Vertex → inlineData).
312	 *
313	 * Why neutral and not provider-shaped here: the route can change between this
314	 * call and actual dispatch (ModelRouter falls back on error). Shaping the image
315	 * for the pre-dispatch provider meant a fallback to a different provider got a
316	 * payload its adapter couldn't read, so the image was silently dropped and the
317	 * model confabulated. Letting the dispatching adapter own the translation makes
318	 * fallback safe.
319	 */
320	function buildVisionMessageContent(
321	  mimeType: string,
322	  base64: string,
323	  requestText: string,
324	): Array<Record<string, unknown>> {
325	  return [
326	    { type: 'text', text: requestText },
327	    { type: 'image', mimeType, data: base64 },
328	  ];
329	}

331	/**
332	 * Pass 1 of image ingestion: send the raw image to the admin-selected
333	 * vision-capable model and get back a faithful text rendering (verbatim text +
334	 * described visual meaning). That text then flows through the SAME chunk →
335	 * extract → validate → promote pipeline as every other document, which keeps
336	 * the quote-validation provenance guarantee intact (claims quote the
337	 * transcription, which is persisted as document_chunks).
338	 *
339	 * The model is chosen in Admin → Settings ("Image vision model") and read here
340	 * via the auxiliary-model registry; the registry's defaultRouteId is only the
341	 * unset/unresolvable fallback.
342	 */
343	async function transcribeImageToText(
344	  client: OracleAIClient,
345	  db: OracleDb,
346	  buffer: Buffer,
347	  doc: { fileName: string; fileType: string; context?: string | null },
348	): Promise<string> {
349	  const resolution = await resolveRouteCandidates(db, 'vision');
350	  for (const skipped of resolution.skipped) {
351	    console.error(
352	      `[document-ingestion] skipped configured vision candidate ${skipped.modelIdOrRouteId}: ${skipped.reason}`,
353	    );
354	  }
355	  const routeCandidates = resolution.candidates;
356	  const route = routeCandidates[0]!.route;
357	  const mimeType = imageMimeFor(doc.fileType, doc.fileName);
358	  const contextLine =
359	    doc.context && doc.context.trim()
360	      ? ` Context from the uploader about this image: "${doc.context.trim()}". Use it to interpret ambiguous labels, but transcribe only what is actually visible - do not add details that are not in the image.`
361	      : '';
362	  const requestText = `Render the attached image "${doc.fileName}" as faithful, complete text.${contextLine}`;
363	
364	  const blocks = [
365	    makeBlock({
366	      id: 'image-vision-system',
367	      label: 'Image vision transcription system prompt',
368	      kind: 'stable_system',
369	      content: IMAGE_TRANSCRIPTION_SYSTEM,
370	      reasonIncluded: 'vision transcription pass for an uploaded image',
371	    }),
372	    makeBlock({
373	      id: 'image-vision-request',
374	      label: 'Image vision transcription request',
375	      kind: 'dynamic_input',
376	      content: requestText,
377	      reasonIncluded: 'dynamic request paired with the inline image',
378	    }),
379	  ];
380	
381	  const callStartedAt = Date.now();
382	  const result = await client.runText({
383	    taskType: 'document_claim_extraction',
384	    routeId: route.routeId,
385	    promptVersion: EXTRACTION_PROMPT_VERSION,
386	    blocks,
387	    providerOptions: {
388	      cache: { disableCache: true },
389	      temperature: VISION_TEMPERATURE,
390	      maxOutputTokens: VISION_MAX_OUTPUT_TOKENS,
391	      highResolutionVision: true,
392	      messages: [
393	        {
394	          role: 'user',
395	          content: buildVisionMessageContent(mimeType, buffer.toString('base64'), requestText),
396	        },
397	      ],
398	    },
399	    routeCandidates,
400	  });
401	
402	  const actualProvider = result.provider ?? route.provider;
403	  const actualModel = result.modelId ?? route.modelId;
404	  const [modelRun] = await db
405	    .insert(modelRuns)
406	    .values({
407	      taskType: 'document-ingestion-vision',
408	      model: actualModel,
409	      provider: actualProvider,
410	      promptVersion: EXTRACTION_PROMPT_VERSION,
411	      inputTokens: result.usage?.inputTokens ?? null,
412	      outputTokens: result.usage?.outputTokens ?? null,
413	      latencyMs: Date.now() - callStartedAt,
414	      success: true,
415	    })
416	    .returning({ id: modelRuns.id })
417	    .catch((e) => {
418	      console.error('[document-ingestion] failed to record vision model_run', e);
419	      return [];
420	    });
421	
422	  if (modelRun?.id) {
423	    await logModelRunAttempts({
424	      db,
425	      metadata: result,
426	      taskType: 'document-ingestion-vision',
427	      slot: 'vision',
428	      modelRunId: modelRun.id,
429	    }).catch((e) =>
430	      console.error('[document-ingestion] failed to record vision model attempts', e),
431	    );
432	  }
433	
434	  return result.text ?? '';
435	}
436	/**
437	 * Decide how to parse a document from its declared MIME type, falling back to
438	 * the filename extension when the browser/OS sends a generic
439	 * `application/octet-stream` (common for .docx on some platforms).
440	 */
441	function resolveParseKind(
442	  fileType: string,
443	  fileName: string,
444	): 'pdf' | 'xlsx' | 'docx' | 'image' | 'text' | 'unsupported' {
445	  const ext = fileName.toLowerCase().split('.').pop() ?? '';
446	  if (fileType === 'application/pdf' || ext === 'pdf') return 'pdf';
447	  if (
448	    fileType === 'application/vnd.ms-excel' ||
449	    fileType === 'application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet' ||
450	    fileType === 'text/csv' ||
451	    ext === 'xlsx' ||
452	    ext === 'xls' ||
453	    ext === 'csv'
454	  ) {
455	    return 'xlsx';
456	  }
457	  if (fileType === DOCX_MIME || ext === 'docx') return 'docx';
458	  // Images go through a vision-model transcription pass. Only the formats
459	  // Gemini accepts natively are routed here; GIF/BMP/TIFF fall through to
460	  // 'unsupported' so we don't hand the model a mime type it rejects.
461	  if (
462	    fileType === 'image/png' ||
463	    fileType === 'image/jpeg' ||
464	    fileType === 'image/jpg' ||
465	    fileType === 'image/webp' ||
466	    fileType === 'image/heic' ||
467	    fileType === 'image/heif' ||
468	    ['png', 'jpg', 'jpeg', 'webp', 'heic', 'heif'].includes(ext)
469	  ) {
470	    return 'image';
471	  }
472	  if (fileType.startsWith('text/') || ['txt', 'md', 'markdown', 'vtt'].includes(ext)) {
473	    return 'text';
474	  }
475	  return 'unsupported';
476	}
477	
478	/**
479	 * Heuristic: does an image transcription look like a diagram (flowchart, swimlane
480	 * map, decision tree) rather than a plain screenshot of prose? The vision prompt
481	 * renders diagrams with `[Lane: "label"]` nodes, `--(Arrow: "…")-->` edges, and
482	 * `### Swimlane` headers, so their presence is a strong signal. Drives a
483	 * relationship-first extraction addendum instead of the box-by-box default.
484	 */
485	function looksLikeDiagramTranscription(text: string): boolean {
486	  return /-->/.test(text) || /--\(Arrow/i.test(text) || /###\s*Swimlane/i.test(text);
487	}
488	
489	/** Map a stored file type / name to a Gemini-accepted image MIME type. */
490	function imageMimeFor(fileType: string, fileName: string): string {
491	  if (fileType.startsWith('image/')) return fileType === 'image/jpg' ? 'image/jpeg' : fileType;
492	  const ext = fileName.toLowerCase().split('.').pop() ?? '';
493	  switch (ext) {
494	    case 'jpg':
495	    case 'jpeg':
496	      return 'image/jpeg';
497	    case 'webp':
498	      return 'image/webp';
499	    case 'heic':
500	      return 'image/heic';
501	    case 'heif':
502	      return 'image/heif';
503	    default:
504	      return 'image/png';
505	  }
506	}

509	// Per-document processor

511	
512	interface ProcessDocumentResult {
513	  chunksInserted: number;
514	  candidatesStaged: number;
515	  claimsPromoted: number;
516	  claimsAutoApproved: number;
517	  duplicatesAppended: number;
518	  rejections: number;
519	}

521	type ExtractedClaim = ExtractionOutput['claims'][number];
522	
523	async function processDocument(
524	  documentId: string,
525	  jobRunId: string,
526	): Promise<ProcessDocumentResult> {
527	  const db = getDirectDb();
528	  const client = buildOracleClient();
529	  const serviceSupabase = createServiceRoleClient();
530	  const outcome: ProcessDocumentResult = {
531	    chunksInserted: 0,
532	    candidatesStaged: 0,
533	    claimsPromoted: 0,
534	    claimsAutoApproved: 0,
535	    duplicatesAppended: 0,
536	    rejections: 0,
537	  };
538	  const documentExtractionCacheSourceHashes = new Set<string>();
539	
540	  // 1. Load + mark processing.
541	  const [doc] = await db.select().from(documents).where(eq(documents.id, documentId)).limit(1);
542	  if (!doc) throw new Error(`Document ${documentId} not found`);
543	  if (doc.status === 'complete') {
544	    console.log(`[document-ingestion] ${documentId} already complete — skipping`);
545	    return outcome;
546	  }
547	  await db
548	    .update(documents)
549	    .set({ status: 'processing', processingError: null, processedAt: null })
550	    .where(eq(documents.id, documentId));
551	
552	  const parseKind = resolveParseKind(doc.fileType, doc.fileName);
553	  const routeCandidates = await resolveExtractionCandidates(db);
554	  const route = routeCandidates[0]!.route;
555	  const activeTopDomainIds = await loadActiveTopDomainIds(db);
556	  const topDomainNameMap = await loadTopDomainNameMap(db);
557	  const entityRegistry = await loadEntityRegistry(db);
558	  const correctionLessons = await loadClaimCorrectionLessonPack(db);
559	
560	  // 2. Download.
561	  const { data: blob, error: downloadError } = await serviceSupabase.storage
562	    .from(doc.storageBucket)
563	    .download(doc.storagePath);
564	  if (downloadError || !blob) {
565	    throw new Error(
566	      `Storage download failed for ${doc.storagePath}: ${downloadError?.message ?? 'empty blob'}`,
567	    );
568	  }
569	  const buffer = Buffer.from(await blob.arrayBuffer());
570	  // The file-backed Vertex cache feeds the raw artifact to the EXTRACTION pass.
571	  // For images that pass runs over the vision transcription (text), not the
572	  // image, so never attach the raw image as a file-backed cache source.
573	  const fileBackedCachePath =
574	    route.provider === 'vertex' &&
575	    parseKind !== 'image' &&
576	    buffer.length > VERTEX_FILE_CACHE_MIN_BYTES
577	      ? await materializeVertexCacheTempFile(buffer, doc.fileName)
578	      : null;
579	
580	  // 3. Parse.
581	  let rawText: string;
582	  try {
583	    if (parseKind === 'pdf') {
584	      rawText = await extractTextFromPdf(buffer);
585	    } else if (parseKind === 'xlsx') {
586	      rawText = await extractTextFromXlsx(buffer);
587	    } else if (parseKind === 'docx') {
588	      rawText = await extractTextFromDocx(buffer);
589	    } else if (parseKind === 'image') {
590	      // Pass 1: vision model renders the image to faithful text. Prefix a
591	      // provenance header so the persisted chunk is self-describing.
592	      const transcription = await transcribeImageToText(client, db, buffer, doc);
593	      rawText = transcription.trim()
594	        ? `Visual transcription of uploaded image "${doc.fileName}" (${doc.fileType}), produced by a vision model because the source is an image rather than text.\n\n${transcription}`
595	        : '';
596	    } else if (parseKind === 'text') {
597	      rawText = buffer.toString('utf-8');
598	    } else {
599	      console.warn(
600	        `[document-ingestion] unsupported file type ${doc.fileType} for doc ${documentId} — marking complete with note`,
601	      );
602	      await db
603	        .update(documents)
604	        .set({
605	          status: 'complete',
606	          processedAt: new Date(),
607	          processingError: `Unsupported file type: ${doc.fileType}`,
608	        })
609	        .where(eq(documents.id, documentId));
610	      if (fileBackedCachePath) {
611	        await unlink(fileBackedCachePath).catch(() => undefined);
612	      }
613	      return outcome;
614	    }
615	  } catch (parseErr) {
616	    if (fileBackedCachePath) {
617	      await unlink(fileBackedCachePath).catch(() => undefined);
618	    }
619	    throw new Error(
620	      `File parse failed for ${doc.fileName}: ${parseErr instanceof Error ? parseErr.message : String(parseErr)}`,
621	    );
622	  }
623	
624	  if (!rawText.trim()) {
625	    await db
626	      .update(documents)
627	      .set({ status: 'complete', processedAt: new Date(), processingError: 'No text extracted' })
628	      .where(eq(documents.id, documentId));
629	    if (fileBackedCachePath) {
630	      await unlink(fileBackedCachePath).catch(() => undefined);
631	    }
632	    return outcome;
633	  }
634	
635	  // 4. Chunk + embed + insert chunks (idempotent dedup by content hash).
636	  //    Structure-aware: cut on headings/paragraphs/lines, never mid-line, so a
637	  //    flowchart arrow line or a sentence is never severed across chunks.
638	  const textChunks = chunkTextStructured(rawText);
639	  let chunkVectors: (number[] | null)[] = [];
640	  // Track degraded outcomes so they are SURFACED on the document, not hidden:
641	  // chunks stored without embeddings are invisible to semantic retrieval, and a
642	  // failed chunk insert means lost text — both previously only console.warn'd.
643	  let embeddingDegraded = false;
644	  let chunkInsertFailures = 0;
645	  try {
646	    const { vectors } = await embedMany(textChunks.map((c) => c.text));
647	    chunkVectors = vectors;
648	  } catch (embedErr) {
649	    embeddingDegraded = true;
650	    console.error(
651	      '[document-ingestion] EMBED FAILED — chunks will be stored WITHOUT vectors and will not be ' +
652	        'retrievable by semantic search until re-embedded:',
653	      embedErr,
654	    );
655	    chunkVectors = textChunks.map(() => null);
656	  }
657	
658	  const insertedChunkIds: InsertedDocumentChunk[] = [];
659	  for (let i = 0; i < textChunks.length; i++) {
660	    const chunkTextWindow = textChunks[i]!;
661	    const contentHash = createHash('sha256').update(chunkTextWindow.text).digest('hex');
662	    try {
663	      const [chunk] = await db
664	        .insert(documentChunks)
665	        .values({
666	          documentId,
667	          chunkIndex: i,
668	          rawText: chunkTextWindow.text,
669	          tokenCount: Math.ceil(chunkTextWindow.text.length / 4),
670	          contentHash,
671	          embedding: chunkVectors[i] ?? null,
672	          metadataJson: { fileType: doc.fileType, fileName: doc.fileName },
673	        })
674	        .onConflictDoNothing()
675	        .returning({ id: documentChunks.id });
676	      if (chunk) {
677	        outcome.chunksInserted += 1;
678	        insertedChunkIds.push({ id: chunk.id, ...chunkTextWindow });
679	      } else {
680	        const [existingChunk] = await db
681	          .select({ id: documentChunks.id })
682	          .from(documentChunks)
683	          .where(
684	            and(
685	              eq(documentChunks.documentId, documentId),
686	              eq(documentChunks.contentHash, contentHash),
687	            ),
688	          )
689	          .limit(1);
690	        if (existingChunk) {
691	          insertedChunkIds.push({ id: existingChunk.id, ...chunkTextWindow });
692	        }
693	      }
694	    } catch (chunkErr) {
695	      chunkInsertFailures += 1;
696	      console.error(`[document-ingestion] chunk ${i} insert failed (text will be lost):`, chunkErr);
697	    }
698	  }
699	
700	  if (insertedChunkIds.length === 0 || rawText.trim().length <= 20) {
701	    await db
702	      .update(documents)
703	      .set({ status: 'complete', processedAt: new Date() })
704	      .where(eq(documents.id, documentId));
705	    if (fileBackedCachePath) {
706	      await unlink(fileBackedCachePath).catch(() => undefined);
707	    }
708	    return outcome;
709	  }
710	
711	  // 5. Process every chunk window. Each model call is bounded, but the
712	  // document itself is not silently truncated.
713	  const extractionWindows = buildDocumentChunkWindows(
714	    insertedChunkIds,
715	    parseKind === 'image' ? MAX_IMAGE_TEXT_CHARS : MAX_DOCUMENT_TEXT_CHARS,
716	  );
717	  const outlineContext = await loadDocumentOutlineContext(db, documentId).catch((err) => {
718	    console.warn(
719	      '[document-ingestion] source outline context unavailable; continuing without it',
720	      err,
721	    );
722	    return null;
723	  });
724	  for (let windowIndex = 0; windowIndex < extractionWindows.length; windowIndex++) {
725	    const extractionWindow = extractionWindows[windowIndex]!;
726	    const windowChunks = extractionWindow.chunks;
727	    const documentCorpus = extractionWindow.content;
728	    const sourceHash = createHash('sha256').update(documentCorpus, 'utf8').digest('hex');
729	    documentExtractionCacheSourceHashes.add(sourceHash);
730	    const [batch] = await db
731	      .insert(extractionBatches)
732	      .values({
733	        jobRunId,
734	        batchType: 'document_page',
735	        status: 'pending_model',
736	        sourceDocumentChunkIds: windowChunks.map((c) => c.id),
737	        sourceHash,
738	        modelRunIdsAttempted: [],
739	        routeIdsAttempted: [route.routeId],
740	      })
741	      .returning({ id: extractionBatches.id });
742	    if (!batch) throw new Error('[document-ingestion] failed to insert extraction_batches row');
743	
744	    // 6. Compile prompt + context pack. The document text becomes the dynamic

756	      makeBlock({
757	        id: 'extraction-system',
758	        label: 'Extraction system prompt + document addendum',
759	        kind: 'stable_system',
760	        content: EXTRACTION_SYSTEM_PROMPT + documentNote,
761	        reasonIncluded: 'extraction prompt v' + EXTRACTION_PROMPT_VERSION + ' (document mode)',

765	            makeBlock({
766	              id: 'reviewer-correction-lessons',
767	              label: 'Approved reviewer correction lessons',
768	              kind: 'semi_stable_domain_context' as const,
769	              content: correctionLessons.promptBlock,
770	              reasonIncluded: 'approved claim revisions teach extraction corrections',
771	            }),
772	          ]
773	        : []),
774	      ...(outlineContext
775	        ? [
776	            makeBlock({

779	              kind: 'semi_stable_domain_context' as const,
780	              content: outlineContext,

783	          ]
784	        : []),
785	      makeBlock({

787	        label: 'Document chunks',
788	        kind: 'retrieved_context',
789	        content: documentCorpus,
790	        reasonIncluded: `window ${windowIndex + 1}/${extractionWindows.length}: ${windowChunks.length} chunks → formatted to ${documentCorpus.length} chars`,
791	      }),
792	      makeBlock({
793	        id: 'document-request',
794	        label: 'Document extraction request',
795	        kind: 'dynamic_input',
796	        content:
797	          'Read the document chunks as a whole, infer the operational process flow they describe, and extract dense, evidence-backed operational claims. For SOP/checklist/responsibility-list text, extract each concrete actionable step or handoff as a separate claim; do not collapse a numbered list or section into one broad summary. Classify by meaning and handoff structure, not by literal keywords. Every exactQuote must be copied verbatim from within ONE provided document chunk, and sourceMessageId must be that exact Document Chunk ID. Return only claims that are explicitly supported by the document text.',
798	        reasonIncluded:
799	          'small dynamic request so the document corpus can be cached as reusable prefix',
800	      }),
801	    ];
802	    const plan = client.compile({
803	      taskType: 'document_claim_extraction',
804	      routeId: route.routeId,
805	      promptVersion: EXTRACTION_PROMPT_VERSION,
806	      blocks,
807	      observability: { includedDocumentChunkIds: windowChunks.map((c) => c.id) },
808	    });
809	
810	    const [contextPack] = await db
811	      .insert(oracleContextPacks)

813	      .returning({ id: oracleContextPacks.id });
814	    if (!contextPack)

816	    await db
817	      .update(extractionBatches)
818	      .set({ contextPackId: contextPack.id })
819	      .where(eq(extractionBatches.id, batch.id));
820	
821	    // 7. Call the model. The adapter now owns the real Vertex explicit-cache
822	    //    lifecycle, including cross-process reuse via provider_cached_content.
823	    let modelOutput: ExtractionOutput | null = null;
824	    let modelRunId: string | null = null;
825	    const callStartedAt = Date.now();
826	    try {
827	      const result = await client.runObject<ExtractionOutput>({
828	        taskType: 'document_claim_extraction',
829	        routeId: route.routeId,
830	        promptVersion: EXTRACTION_PROMPT_VERSION,
831	        blocks,
832	        schema: ExtractionOutputSchema,
833	        observability: { includedDocumentChunkIds: windowChunks.map((c) => c.id) },
834	        providerOptions: {
835	          maxOutputTokens: EXTRACTION_MAX_OUTPUT_TOKENS,
836	          cache: {
837	            preferLongLivedCache: true,
838	            preferExplicitCache: route.provider === 'vertex',

1	# AGENT_ERROR_LOG.md — runtime failures for a coding agent to fix
2	
3	## What this file is (read first)
4	
5	This is the **designated place where runtime failures are written so a coding AI agent (or human) can act on them.** The Oracle's background workers run on Trigger.dev and cannot write into this repo, so a failure that only lands in Trigger.dev's dashboard or a `model_run_attempts` row is, in practice, **failing silently** — nobody and no agent is watching those. This file is the durable, in-repo, agent-readable digest.
6	
7	**Hard rule (design phase):** if a worker/run fails, it must end up here, in a form specific enough to change code from — exact error signature, where it surfaced, root cause with `file:line`, evidence (run IDs / DB counts), the fix, and status. `HANDOFF.md` links here so every session sees it.
8	
9	### How entries get here (until fully automated)
10	
11	1. **Now (manual/session):** when a run is found failed (Trigger MCP `list_runs`/`get_run_details`, or DB `job_runs`/`model_run_attempts`), append/refresh an entry below.
12	2. **Required enabling work (see fix_enhancement.md §5 Bug C / priority 1):** the macro/coverage/outline workers must write `job_runs` rows and a document-level `macro_health` status. Once they do, `scripts/export-worker-failures.mjs` (to be written) can query recent failed `job_runs` + `model_run_attempts` and refresh the "Open failures" section automatically. **Until that lands, absence of an entry here does NOT mean absence of failure — check Trigger.dev and DB counts too.**
13	
14	### Entry status legend
15	
16	- **OPEN** — still failing in the current deployed worker.
17	- **MITIGATED** — a code/config change addresses it but is not yet verified in prod (needs deploy/migration/re-run).
18	- **FIXED** — verified not failing after the change (record the evidence).
19	
20	---
21	
22	## Open / recent failures
23	
24	### ERR-001 — Macro & coverage workers hard-fail: `AllCandidatesFailedError`  — ✅ FIXED & VERIFIED IN PROD (2026-07-03)

26	**RESOLUTION (verified 2026-07-03, worker `20260703.4`):** macro + coverage now succeed in prod — `macro_relationships` inserted, `source_coverage_findings = 4`, `documents.macro_health = complete`, macro attempt `success openai/gpt-4.1-mini primary=true`. Two things had to be fixed together:
27	1. The model route (below) — moved off the Qwen `general` slot to a dedicated `macro` slot **with a fallback pool**.
28	2. **A second, hidden discovery from the fallback logs: Gemini also can't do it.** `google/gemini-2.5-flash` AND `gemini-2.5-pro` both reject the nested macro schemas with `400 "The specified schema produces a constraint that is too complex"`. So the *first* seeded primary (Gemini) would have hard-failed too — the fallback pool saved the run by falling through to `openai/gpt-4.1-mini`, which handles strict complex schemas. **The primary was corrected to `openai/gpt-4.1-mini`** (settings, migration 83/84, seed). Lesson: for the macro schemas, use an **OpenAI** strict-json-schema model; Gemini is only safe for the simpler source-outline schema.
29	This is also the proof that the fallback-pool design (below) is load-bearing, not optional — both the Qwen primary and the Gemini primary failed; only the pool made the layer resilient.

61	### ERR-002 — Support-claim SQL queries fail: `42P10 SELECT DISTINCT / ORDER BY`  — ✅ FIXED & VERIFIED (2026-07-03)
62	
63	- **Symptom:** `macro-relationship-extraction` (and `source-coverage-audit` when a document was linked) failed fast, before the model call, with `Error: Failed query:`.
64	- **Root cause (reproduced directly against prod):** `42P10 — for SELECT DISTINCT, ORDER BY expressions must appear in select list`. **Three** queries used `SELECT DISTINCT … ORDER BY c.created_at` (cross-source also `ORDER BY CASE …`) without those in the select list: the cross-source CTE and single-source query in `macro-relationship-extraction.ts`, and the claim query in `source-coverage-audit.ts`. So the document-scoped macro path was **always** broken, independent of ERR-001. (My earlier "likely fixed in `.2`" guess was wrong — it only "reached the model" on runs where `documentId` was null and the query was skipped.)
65	- **Fix:** cross-source → drop the redundant outer `DISTINCT` (the `IN`-subquery already yields unique ids); single-source + coverage → add `c.created_at` to the select list (JOINs can duplicate, so `DISTINCT` stays). All three corrected forms verified against prod (40/40/80 rows) before patching, then verified live (post-fix macro/coverage runs completed — see ERR-001).
66	- **Do next (optional):** a DB-touching smoke test that runs all three queries against a seeded fixture so this class can't silently regress.

68	### ERR-003 — Followup fan-out: each lens job re-triggers macro + coverage  — MITIGATED IN SOURCE (needs deploy/rerun verification)
69	
70	- **Symptom:** one `source-outline` run produced **~49 macro + ~50 coverage** runs in ~15 min (`job_runs`). Pre-fix those were failures; post-fix they'd be redundant *successes* over the same claims.
71	- **Cause:** both `source-outline` AND every `document-lens-extraction` job trigger the two followups on completion. With coverage-first fan-out dispatching up to 16 lens jobs, that's up to ~16× redundant macro/coverage passes (the near-duplicate guard prevents duplicate rows, but the model calls are wasted spend + noise, and they leave `macro_health='degraded'` from any transient failure).
72	- **Fix applied in source:** `document-lens-extraction` no longer triggers macro + coverage directly. Lens jobs now record completed/skipped-existing work, and the final completed lens job claims a one-time `source_outlines.budget_json.macroFollowupsDispatchedAt` latch before triggering `macro-relationship-extraction`. `macro-relationship-extraction` triggers `source-coverage-audit` only after the macro pass finishes, so coverage no longer races ahead of relationship insertion. Verify after deploy by rerunning a diagram outline and checking for one macro run plus one coverage run per outline.
